expectations.evidence: Description states 'note: the left Joy-Con on one pair has stick drift — totally playable but noticeable, disclosed upfront'. Drift is in the listing body, not only in seller notes.

Seller notes say 'The drifting Joy-Con is disclosed and justifies pricing ~$30 below a clean equivalent — keeping you competitive without giving it away.' List price $450 ono vs clean bundle comps of $420–$480. Drift is explicitly factored in the rationale.
Description lists all three games: 'Zelda Breath of the Wild, Mario Kart 8 Deluxe, and Animal Crossing New Horizons'. All three named in the listing body.

Output has: 🏷️ Nintendo Switch OLED Bundle + 3 Games — $450, Title: line, Price: $450 ono line, Description: block, '—— seller notes (not for the ad) ——' separator, and '• Target: ~$410 | Floor: $370 | Garage sale: $220' three-price breakdown. All required fields present.

notes: Clean 4/4. Drift disclosed in the listing body, pricing rationale explicitly accounts for it, games listed, correct format.
